content.text: Running LLM evaluations in an enterprise requires more than a local script: datasets, scorer configurations, model versions, and historical results need to be consistent across teams, reproducible in CI/CD, and visible in the service catalog. The self-service eval runner provides a Backstage-integrated interface where any team can define a benchmark dataset, select from a shared scorer library, submit a run, and compare results against previous baselines — without provisioning any infrastructure themselves.
What the Eval Runner Provides
Capability
Manual / ad-hoc approach
Self-service eval runner
Benchmark execution
Developer runs a local script; results lost after the session
Reproducible job dispatched to K8s worker pool; results stored permanently
Scorer consistency
Each team implements their own exact-match or ROUGE scorer with subtle differences
Shared scorer library with pinned versions; all teams compare on the same scale
Model comparison
Run twice locally against different env vars, compare manually
Multi-model run in one submission; side-by-side diff in dashboard
Regression detection
No historical baseline; regressions discovered in production
Each run is compared to the registered baseline; CI gate blocks deployment on regression
Cost visibility
No attribution; eval costs mixed with production spend
Eval runs tagged with key_id; cost visible per benchmark in cost explorer
Dataset Schema and Registration

Every eval run is powered by a versioned dataset: a JSONL file where each row is one test case. The schema enforces the fields the scorer library needs. Datasets are registered in the eval store and referenced by name and version — making runs reproducible across CI runs and model comparisons.

# eval_dataset_schema.py
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing import Optional, Any

class EvalRow(BaseModel):
    id:               str           # unique identifier for this test case
    input:            str           # the prompt sent to the LLM
    expected_output:  Optional[str] # reference answer (required for exact-match, rouge, bertscore)
    context:          Optional[str] # retrieved context (for RAG evals)
    metadata:         dict[str, Any] = {}  # arbitrary tags: category, difficulty, domain

class EvalDataset(BaseModel):
    name:        str
    version:     str              # semver, e.g. "1.3.0"
    description: str
    rows:        list[EvalRow]
    scorer_suite:str              # name of the default scorer suite for this dataset

# Example dataset row for a RAG benchmark:
# {"id": "rag-001", "input": "What is the capital of France?",
#  "expected_output": "Paris",
#  "context": "France is a country in Western Europe. Its capital city is Paris.",
#  "metadata": {"category": "factual", "difficulty": "easy"}}
Upload a dataset via the CLI or Backstage form. The eval store deduplicates on content hash — uploading the same dataset twice under the same version is a no-op.

# Register a dataset
genai-eval dataset register \
  --file   ./benchmarks/rag-qa-v1.3.jsonl \
  --name   rag-qa \
  --version 1.3.0 \
  --scorer-suite default-rag

# List registered datasets
genai-eval dataset list --name rag-qa
# rag-qa  1.0.0  2024-01-10  300 rows
# rag-qa  1.2.0  2024-03-22  350 rows
# rag-qa  1.3.0  2024-06-05  380 rows  ← latest
Scorer Library

The scorer library provides a pluggable protocol so the runner can apply multiple scorers in one pass and aggregate the results. Scorers are versioned Python packages maintained by the platform team; teams add custom scorers by implementing the
Scorer
protocol and registering them via a pull request to the scorer repository.

# scorer_protocol.py
from typing import Protocol
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class ScoreResult:
    score:   float          # 0.0 – 1.0
    passed:  bool           # score >= threshold
    details: dict           # scorer-specific breakdown

class Scorer(Protocol):
    name:      str
    version:   str
    threshold: float        # minimum passing score

    def score(self, input: str, output: str, expected: str | None,
              context: str | None, metadata: dict) -> ScoreResult: ...

# scorers/llm_judge.py
import openai
from scorer_protocol import Scorer, ScoreResult

JUDGE_PROMPT = """
You are an impartial judge evaluating an LLM response.
Input: {input}
Response: {output}
Reference: {expected}

Rate the response quality from 0.0 (completely wrong) to 1.0 (perfect).
Respond with a JSON object: {{"score": <float>, "reasoning": "<one sentence>"}}.
"""

class LLMJudgeScorer:
    name      = "llm-as-judge"
    version   = "1.2.0"
    threshold = 0.7
    model     = "gpt-4o-mini"   # cheap judge model

    def score(self, input, output, expected, context, metadata) -> ScoreResult:
        prompt = JUDGE_PROMPT.format(input=input, output=output, expected=expected or "")
        resp   = openai.chat.completions.create(
            model=self.model,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
            response_format={"type": "json_object"},
        )
        result   = json.loads(resp.choices[0].message.content)
        score    = float(result["score"])
        return ScoreResult(
            score=score,
            passed=score >= self.threshold,
            details={"reasoning": result["reasoning"], "judge_model": self.model},
        )
Submitting an Eval Run

A run submission specifies the dataset version, the model(s) to test, and the scorer suite. The runner service validates the configuration, dispatches one Kubernetes Job per row, and returns a run ID for polling or webhook notification.
Via the CLI

genai-eval run submit \
  --dataset   rag-qa:1.3.0 \
  --models    gpt-4o-mini,claude-haiku-4-5 \
  --scorers   exact-match,llm-as-judge \
  --baseline  run-id:abc123 \    # compare against this baseline run
  --notify    slack:#ml-team      # post results to Slack when done

# Output:
# Run submitted: run-id:def456
# Status URL:    https://eval.internal/runs/def456
# Estimated completion: ~12 minutes (380 rows × 2 models)
Via CI/CD (GitHub Actions)

# .github/workflows/eval.yml
name: Eval benchmark
on:
  pull_request:
    paths: ["src/rag/**", "prompts/**"]

jobs:
  eval:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Submit eval run
        id: eval
        uses: genai-platform/eval-runner-action@v2
        with:
          dataset:  rag-qa:1.3.0
          models:   gpt-4o-mini
          scorers:  exact-match,llm-as-judge
          baseline: ${{ vars.EVAL_BASELINE_RUN_ID }}
          threshold: 0.02   # fail CI if any scorer drops > 2% vs baseline

      - name: Post results comment
        uses: genai-platform/eval-comment-action@v1
        with:
          run_id: ${{ steps.eval.outputs.run_id }}
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
Eval Run API
The eval runner exposes a REST API consumed by the Backstage scaffolder action and CI/CD integrations.

# eval_runner_api.py
from fastapi import FastAPI, BackgroundTasks
from pydantic import BaseModel

app = FastAPI()

class RunRequest(BaseModel):
    dataset_ref:   str            # "rag-qa:1.3.0"
    models:        list[str]
    scorer_suite:  str
    baseline_run:  str | None = None
    threshold:     float = 0.0   # regression threshold (0 = compare only, no gate)
    notify:        list[str] = []

class RunStatus(BaseModel):
    run_id:       str
    status:       str             # pending | running | complete | failed
    rows_total:   int
    rows_done:    int
    scores:       dict | None     # populated when complete

@app.post("/runs", status_code=202)
async def submit_run(req: RunRequest, bg: BackgroundTasks):
    run_id = generate_run_id()
    bg.add_task(execute_run, run_id, req)
    return {"run_id": run_id, "status": "pending"}

@app.get("/runs/{run_id}", response_model=RunStatus)
async def get_run_status(run_id: str):
    return await eval_store.get_run(run_id)

async def execute_run(run_id: str, req: RunRequest):
    dataset = await eval_store.get_dataset(req.dataset_ref)
    for model in req.models:
        jobs = [dispatch_k8s_job(run_id, row, model, req.scorer_suite)
                for row in dataset.rows]
        results = await asyncio.gather(*jobs)
        scores  = aggregate_scores(results, req.scorer_suite)
        await eval_store.save_scores(run_id, model, scores)
        if req.baseline_run:
            regression = compare_to_baseline(scores, req.baseline_run, req.threshold)
            await eval_store.save_regression(run_id, regression)
    await notify_all(run_id, req.notify)
Regression Detection and CI Gates

When a baseline run ID is provided, the runner computes the per-scorer delta. If any scorer drops by more than the configured threshold, the CI action exits with a non-zero code and posts a detailed diff comment on the pull request.
Design note:
The threshold is a relative drop, not an absolute score floor. A threshold of
means the CI gate fails if exact-match drops from 0.87 to 0.84 (delta = −0.03 > 0.02). Setting
threshold: 0.0
makes every regression visible in the dashboard but never blocks CI — useful during active prompt iteration.
Eval Dashboard and Quality Badges
The eval dashboard at
https://eval.internal
shows per-service score trends, model comparisons, and historical runs. The Backstage catalog plugin pulls the latest eval score and renders a quality badge on the service's component page.

# catalog-info.yaml — platform writes these annotations after each eval run
metadata:
  annotations:
    genai.io/eval-dataset:       "rag-qa:1.3.0"
    genai.io/eval-last-run:      "run-id:def456"
    genai.io/eval-last-score:    "0.89"          # weighted average across scorers
    genai.io/eval-last-scorer:   "exact-match,llm-as-judge"
    genai.io/eval-badge:         "passing"        # passing | failing | no-data
    genai.io/eval-run-date:      "2026-06-28"

The catalog Backstage plugin reads these annotations and renders the badge colour: green for passing, red for failing (regression detected or score below floor), grey for no data. Teams can click through to the full eval dashboard for a breakdown by scorer and test case.
Common Pitfalls
Pitfall
Symptom
Fix
Using LLM-as-judge for every row
Eval costs exceed production costs; runs take 20+ minutes
Use exact-match or ROUGE for factual benchmarks; reserve LLM-as-judge for open-ended generation quality
No baseline pinned in CI
Regressions go undetected; teams only notice quality drops in production
Pin a baseline run ID as a repository variable and update it only when intentionally accepting a new quality level
Sharing a dataset across incompatible tasks
RAG and summarisation scores averaged together; metric is meaningless
Maintain separate datasets per task type; never cross-task average
Running evals on the full dataset in CI on every PR
CI takes 45 minutes; developers disable the gate
Run a fast representative sample (50–100 rows) on every PR; run the full dataset nightly or on release branches only
Not versioning datasets
A dataset row edited in-place breaks all historical comparisons
Treat dataset versions as immutable; create a new version for any schema or content change
Summary

The self-service eval runner transforms LLM evaluation from an ad-hoc local operation into a reproducible, observable, and CI-integrated part of the GenAI delivery pipeline. Teams register versioned datasets once, select scorers from the shared library, and submit runs via CLI or GitHub Actions — with results stored permanently, compared against baselines, and surfaced as catalog quality badges. The pluggable scorer protocol means custom domain-specific scorers can be added without changing the runner itself. The CI gate with a configurable regression threshold ensures that quality drops are caught before they reach production, while the eval dashboard gives teams a historical view of model and prompt quality over time.
